svg.title: Full FPGA cloud stack with DigitalOcean Droplet
svg.desc: End-to-end diagram from user browser through DigitalOcean Droplet, Tailscale tunnel, host machine, USB hub for JTAG, ethernet switch, and 10 FPGAs. Each component has a description.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does a user need to access the FPGA cloud API?')
g.text: Any browser or HTTP client (curl, Python, etc.)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What exactly runs on the DigitalOcean Droplet?')
g.text: A small rented Linux VPS with a real public IP address.
Runs nginx as a reverse proxy. Terminates HTTPS/TLS.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What software runs on the host machine?')
g.text: Your physical server or PC in the lab. Runs Tailscale so
the Droplet can reach it privately. Runs your API server
(e.g. FastAPI in Python) that handles all user requests.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why do I need a USB hub for 10 FPGAs?')
sendPrompt('What kind of ethernet switch do I need for 10 FPGAs?')
g.text: and all FPGAs share one LAN.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does each FPGA get a static IP on the local network?')
sendPrompt('How does each FPGA get a static IP on the local network?')
sendPrompt('How does each FPGA get a static IP on the local network?')
sendPrompt('How does each FPGA get a static IP on the local network?')
sendPrompt('How does each FPGA get a static IP on the local network?')

svg.text: Each FPGA runs two things simultaneously in its fabric:

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is a soft ethernet MAC core and how does it work on the ECP5?')
g.text: LiteEth or similar IP core. Gives

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the user design that runs on the FPGA?')
g.text: by the user. Does the real work.

svg.text: HTTPS — user to Droplet (public internet, encrypted)
Tailscale VPN — Droplet to host (private, encrypted tunnel)
JTAG / USB — host to FPGAs (programming only)
